title: Create SQLite database schema for weight_entries table
description: Define and initialize the weight_entries table using SQLAlchemy ORM. This table stores all weight log entries per user.
acceptanceCriteria: weight_entries table exists with all required columns
id column is a UUID string primary key
user_id column is a foreign key referencing users.id with ON DELETE CASCADE
weight_value column is a Float and must be positive (validated at API layer)
recorded_at column is a Date (not DateTime) — stores only the calendar date
created_at column defaults to current UTC datetime
Table is created automatically on app startup if it doesn't exist

steps.details: Create src/api/models/weight_entry.py. Define class WeightEntry(Base) with __tablename__ = 'weight_entries'. Columns: id (String primary key, default=lambda: str(uuid.uuid4())), user_id (String, ForeignKey('users.id', ondelete='CASCADE'), nullable=False, index=True), weight_value (Float, nullable=False), recorded_at (Date, nullable=False), created_at (DateTime, default=datetime.utcnow). Add relationship back to User model.

steps.description: Register the model with Base metadata
steps.details: In src/api/main.py (or a models __init__.py), import WeightEntry so SQLAlchemy registers it with Base.metadata before create_all() is called. Without this import, the table won't be created.

steps.description: Verify table creation and foreign key
steps.details: Run the app and verify: 'weight_entries' table exists in db.sqlite, run 'PRAGMA table_info(weight_entries)' to confirm columns, run 'PRAGMA foreign_key_list(weight_entries)' to confirm FK to users.id with CASCADE.

technicalNotes: SQLite foreign key enforcement requires 'PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON' to be set per connection — add this to SQLAlchemy engine events if cascade deletes are critical
Use Date type (not DateTime) for recorded_at since we only store the calendar date of the entry
Index user_id for faster queries when filtering entries by user
